programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Painting: Mixed Media (2SLGBTQ+)
programs.description: Create pictures with a variety of paint types. From oil and acrylic to tempera and watercolours, learn how and when to use different paints to create specific effects.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pilates With Baby
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being designed specifically for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: Pottery
programs.description: Create with clay. Learn the basics of pottery including how to shape and sculpt with your hands and tools to create and finish 3D forms.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Recreation Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Jump into the adventures of science. Here, stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play and the use of your imagination will help you to explore science mysteries. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Jump into the adventures of science. Here, stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play and the use of your imagination will help you to explore science mysteries. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Discovery
programs.description: Do you have a future scientist in the family? If so, come learn about scientific theories and concepts and how to conduct experiments. In this activity, you will also get answers about the world around you and build on basics by exploring chemical reactions, flight dynamics, cellular mechanics and the inner workings of the human body.

programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning

programs.name: Swim Clinic: Strokes - Legs/Kicking Techniques
programs.description: Are you looking to improve a specific swim skill with focused classes? With the goal of improving kicking skills and techniques, you will develop the flutter kick, whip kick, eggbeater, scissor kick and dolphin kick to effectively move in the water. Offered in both English and Ukrainian.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Visual Diary & Self-Reflection
programs.description: Discover the  power of visual journaling as a tool for self-reflection and creativity. Through  drawing, collage and mixed media, tap into your inner world and reconnect with  your authentic self – no art experience required. Guided prompts and intuitive  exercises help you build a regular creative journaling practice, break through  self-censorship and express freely without worrying about perfectionism.  Inspired by the instructor’s own journey as a migrant woman, this course offers  a playful yet meaningful path to creative growth.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Latin
programs.description: Calling all Latin dance lovers, this is your chance to try the tango, rumba, cha-cha and mambo. Learn basic steps, postures and holds through various exercises and engaging choreography. This is not a couples class

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.
programs.location: Gord and Irene Risk Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance - Advanced
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Building on basic steps and combinations, and with a focus on moving with confidence, you will learn more challenging choreography, including progressive turns, ball changes and new formations.

programs.name: Dodgeball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play recreational badminton. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Dodgeball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play recreational badminton. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.
programs.location: Topham Park Cmty Centre and Clubhouse

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Flag Football: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In flag football, a non-contact version of tackle football where flags are used to stop a play, you will learn basics like throwing, catching and offensive and defensive plays as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Functional Mobility: TIME
programs.description: In partnership with University Health Network, Toronto Rehabilitation Institute, a modified exercise program suitable for adults with mobility challenges, who can walk at least 10 metres and may use a cane or walker. Exercises are designed to increase strength, balance, mobility and overall health. Participant screening is required prior to registration, please contact Pauline Phillips at 416 395 0267 before registering for this program.You may bring a support person with you. FitnessTO memberships do not apply.Participants Must Be Able To:1. Walk 10 meters (2 lane residential street) without the help of another person. -Can use other kind of aide. -Speed and quality is not important 2. Follow instructions and demonstrations in small group setting.3. Safely stand and move with hand support for balance. 4. Rise from a chair on their own. For more information, registration assistance, or access to subsidy support, please contact 437-331-6325.www.uhn.ca/TorontoRehab/Clinics/TIME

programs.description: Seeking a class with a more relaxed pace to improve your fitness? Using gentle movements and low-intensity level exercises, workouts will include both  cardio and muscle conditioning options.

programs.name: Golf: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In golf, you will learn basics like stance, swing, putt and drive as well as game etiquette through drills, practice and play.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Hair: Braiding, Weaving and Wigs
programs.description: Here's a skill you can put to work—hair braiding, weaving and wig making. Instruction will focus on the basics of working with hair as well as a variety of techniques.Equipment: You must bring your own hair and hair extensions.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate -Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Mobility and Strength: Chair
programs.description: Improving your mobility and strength can help make day-to-day activities easier.  With a focus on balance and coordination you will use a chair or your walker to complete basic movements in a safe and engaging environment.

programs.name: Multi Arts
programs.description: Multi  Arts looks to explore the world of Art. Here you will work with a variety of  materials and mediums to learn and practice different styles and techniques.  Whether you are drawing, painting, sketching, or sculpting, you will have the  opportunity to create unique art pieces.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Extended Hours: PM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care after the end of the camp day for children registered in City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp. Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Flag Football: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In flag football, a non-contact version of tackle football where flags are used to stop a play, you will learn basics like throwing, catching and offensive and defensive plays as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Functional Mobility: TIME
programs.description: In partnership with University Health Network, Toronto Rehabilitation Institute, a modified exercise program suitable for adults with mobility challenges, who can walk at least 10 metres and may use a cane or walker. Exercises are designed to increase strength, balance, mobility and overall health. Participant screening is required prior to registration, please contact Pauline Phillips at 416 395 0267 before registering for this program.You may bring a support person with you. FitnessTO memberships do not apply.Participants Must Be Able To:1. Walk 10 meters (2 lane residential street) without the help of another person. -Can use other kind of aide. -Speed and quality is not important 2. Follow instructions and demonstrations in small group setting.3. Safely stand and move with hand support for balance. 4. Rise from a chair on their own. For more information, registration assistance, or access to subsidy support, please contact 437-331-6325.www.uhn.ca/TorontoRehab/Clinics/TIME

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pottery
programs.description: Create with clay. Learn the basics of pottery including how to shape and sculpt with your hands and tools to create and finish 3D forms.

programs.name: Qigong
programs.description: Take part in a series of mind and body exercises to improve your flexibility, strength and posture. With focused intention and relaxed movement, you will learn how to integrate breathing and meditation into your wellness routine.

programs.name: Ranger Patrol
programs.description: The Ranger Patrol program focuses on performance with a 200-metre timed swim and 100-metre fitness medley. It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Recreation Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Rookie Patrol
programs.description: The Rookie Patrol program focuses on performance with a 100-metre timed swim and 350-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on assessing conscious victims, treatment of bleeding, and contacting Emergency Medical Services (EMS).Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Do you have a future scientist in the family? If so, come learn about scientific theories and concepts and how to conduct experiments. In this activity, you will also get answers about the world around you and build on basics by exploring chemical reactions, flight dynamics, cellular mechanics and the inner workings of the human body. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Sculpture
programs.description: Create in 3D! Using a variety of materials like clay, plasticine, paper mache and other recycled materials you will learn to mold, form, carve and caste to create beautiful art.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Multi Arts
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.    **WAS FORMERLY CALLED MIXED MEDIA

programs.name: Multi Arts
programs.description: Multi Arts looks to explore the world of Art. Here you will work with a variety of materials and mediums to learn and practice different styles and techniques. Whether you are drawing, painting, sketching, or sculpting, you will have the opportunity to create unique art pieces.

programs.name: Multi Arts
programs.description: Multi Arts looks to explore the world of Art. Here you will work with a variety of materials and mediums to learn and practice different styles and techniques. Whether you are drawing, painting, sketching, or sculpting, you will have the opportunity to create unique art pieces.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional    sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while    improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In    multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer,    volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must    actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Musical Theatre
programs.description: Combining music, spoken dialogue and dance, musical theatre is a great way to explore the arts. Through a variety of exercises and activities, you will build the confidence to take the stage to sing, dance and act.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Osteo Fit
programs.description: Seeking a class with a more relaxed pace to improve your fitness? Using gentle movements and low-intensity level exercises, workouts will include both  cardio and muscle conditioning options.

programs.name: Painting for Absolute Beginners
programs.description: Learn the basics of acrylic painting in this beginner's workshop, designed to give students a basic knowledge of materials and methods. Through demonstrations and a series of exercises, students will paint a picture from start to finish, learn colour theory, and apply art principles towards their creations. Critical advice will be provided as works are in progress. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Performing Arts with Caregiver
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills. Caregiver must be present.

programs.name: Photography
programs.description: Discover the world using a camera lens. Learn tips and tricks on how to use a camera as well as basic photography concepts like use of light and shade, composition, and the rule of thirds. You will also learn how to modify and create photos in various environments.Equipment: You must bring your own devices.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Pickleball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
